Generalized Context Model:
### Generalized Context Model

**Reference:** [4]

**Experiments:**
- Shepard categorization
- Medin categorization
- Weather prediction task

**Log-likelihood:**

$$
 p(c_t = i \mid x_t) \propto \exp\left( \beta \sum_{k=1}^{t-1} \exp(-\|x_k - x_t\|^2) \cdot \mathbb{1}[y_k = i] \right)
$$

where $x_t$ denotes the feature vector of the item at trial $t$, and $y_t$ is the corresponding class label. $\beta$ is a free parameter.
Prospect Theory Model:
### Prospect Theory Model

**Reference:** [5]

**Experiments:**
- CPC18
- choices13k
- Decisions from description

**Log-likelihood:**

$$
 p(c_t = i \mid p_i, x_i) \propto \exp\left( \exp(\beta)\, \pi(p_i)^\top u(x_i) \right)
$$

Probability weighting:

$$
 \pi(p_i) = \sigma(a) + \sigma(b) \cdot p_i
$$

Utility function:

$$
 u(x_i) =
 \begin{cases}
  \sigma(c) \cdot x_i^{\sigma(d)}, & x_i \ge 0 \\
  -\sigma(e) \cdot (-x_i)^{\sigma(g)}, & x_i < 0
 \end{cases}
$$

Free parameters: $\beta, a, b, c, d, e, f, g$.
Hyperbolic Discounting Model:
### Hyperbolic Discounting Model

**Reference:** [6]

**Experiments:**
- Intertemporal choice

**Log-likelihood:**

$$
 p(c_t = i \mid x_i, \gamma_i) \propto \exp\left( \beta \cdot \frac{x_i}{1 + a\gamma_i} \right)
$$

where $x_i$ is the reward magnitude and $\gamma_i$ is the delay. Free parameters: $\beta, a$.
Dual-Systems Model:
### Dual-Systems Model

**Reference:** [7]

**Experiments:**
- Two-step task

**Log-likelihood:**

$$
 p(c_t = i \mid s_t = s) \propto
 \begin{cases}
  \exp\left( \beta [ \sigma(\tau) Q^{MB}_{s,i} + (1-\sigma(\tau)) Q^{MF}_{s,i} ] \right), & s = 0 \\
  \exp\left( \beta Q^{MF}_{s,i} \right), & s > 0
 \end{cases}
$$

$Q^{MB}$ and $Q^{MF}$ denote model-based and model-free value estimates. Free parameters: $\beta, \tau$. A first-stage stickiness term is included but omitted for brevity.
Rescorla-Wagner Model:
### Rescorla–Wagner Model

**Reference:** [8]

**Experiments:**
- Drifting four-armed bandit
- Horizon task
- Two-armed bandit
- Probabilistic instrumental learning
- Iowa gambling task
- Changing bandit
- Decisions from experience

**Log-likelihood:**

$$
 p(c_t = i) \propto \exp(a V_{i,t} + b S_{i,t} + c I_{i,t})
$$

Value update:

$$
 V_{i,t} =
 \begin{cases}
  V_{i,t-1} + \sigma(\alpha^+) (r_{t-1} - V_{i,t-1}), & r_{t-1} - V_{i,t-1} \ge 0 \\
  V_{i,t-1} + \sigma(\alpha^-) (r_{t-1} - V_{i,t-1}), & r_{t-1} - V_{i,t-1} < 0 \\
  V_{i,t-1}, & \text{otherwise}
 \end{cases}
$$

Additional terms:
- $S_{i,t} = \mathbb{1}[c_{t-1}=i]$
- $I_{i,t} = \sum_{k=1}^{t-1} \mathbb{1}[c_k=i]$

Free parameters: $\alpha^+, \alpha^-, a, b, c, d$.
Rescorla-Wagner Model with Context:
### Rescorla–Wagner Model with Context

**Reference:** [9]

**Experiments:**
- Conditional associative learning

**Log-likelihood:**

$$
 p(c_t = i \mid s_t = s) \propto \exp(\beta V_{s,i,t})
$$

Value update:

$$
 V_{s,i,t} =
 \begin{cases}
  V_{s,i,t-1} + \sigma(\alpha)(r_{t-1}-V_{s,i,t-1}), & c_{t-1}=i, s_{t-1}=s \\
  V_{s,i,t-1}, & \text{otherwise}
 \end{cases}
$$

Free parameters: $\alpha, \beta, d$.
Linear Regression Model:
### Linear Regression Model

**Reference:** [10]

**Experiments:**
- Multiple-cue judgment
- Gardening task

**Log-likelihood (multiple-cue judgment):**

$$
 p(c_t=i \mid x_t) \propto \exp( \beta w_t^\top x_t - i^2 + \gamma )
$$

**Log-likelihood (gardening task):**

$$
 p(c_t=\text{accept} \mid x_t) \propto \exp(\beta w_t^\top x_t), \quad p(c_t=\text{reject}) \propto 1
$$

Learning rule:

$$
 w_t = w_{t-1} + \alpha (r_{t-1} - w_{t-1}^\top x_{t-1}) x_{t-1}
$$

Free parameters: $\alpha, \beta, \gamma, d$.
Weighted-Additive Model:
### Weighted-Additive Model

**Reference:** [11]

**Experiments:**
- Multi-attribute decision-making

**Log-likelihood:**

$$
 p(c_t=i \mid x_i) \propto \exp(w^\top x_i)
$$

where $x_i$ is the feature vector of option $i$. $w$ are free parameters.
Decision-Updated Reference Point Model:
### Decision-Updated Reference Point Model

**Reference:** [12]

**Experiments:**
- Columbia card task

**Log-likelihood:**

$$
 p(c_t=\text{sample}) \propto \exp(h(x_{win}p_{win}+x_{loss}p_{loss})+i), \quad p(c_t=\text{stop}) \propto \exp(j)
$$

Probability weighting and utility functions follow prospect theory. Free parameters: $a,b,c,d,e,f,g,h,i,j$.
Odd-One-Out Model:
### Odd-One-Out Model

**Reference:** [13]

**Experiments:**
- THINGS odd-one-out

**Log-likelihood:**

$$
 p(c_t=i \mid x_i,x_j,x_k) \propto \exp(x_j^\top x_k)
$$

where object embeddings $x_i,x_j,x_k \in \mathbb{R}^{16}$ are free parameters.
Multi-Task Reinforcement Learning Model:
### Multi-Task Reinforcement Learning Model

**Reference:** [14]

**Experiments:**
- Multi-task reinforcement learning
- Zoopermarket

This model jointly learns across multiple tasks using shared representations. Parameterization and likelihood follow [14].
GP-UCB Model:
### GP-UCB Model

**Reference:** [15]

**Experiments:**
- Spatially correlated multi-armed bandit
- Structured bandit

**Log-likelihood:**

$$
 p(c_t=i) \propto \exp(\beta(m_{i,t}+\exp(\gamma)s_{i,t}))
$$

where $m_{i,t}$ and $s_{i,t}$ are GP posterior mean and variance. Free parameters: $\beta, \gamma$.
Rational Model:
### Rational Model

**Reference:** N/A

**Experiments:**
- Balloon analog risk task
- N-back
- Digit span
- Go/no-go
- Recent probes
- Serial reaction time task

**Log-likelihood:**

$$
 p(c_t=i \mid o_t=j) \propto \exp(\Theta_{j,i})
$$

where $j$ is the optimal choice. $\Theta \in \mathbb{R}^{N_c \times N_c}$ are free parameters.
Lookup Table Model:
### Lookup Table Model

**Reference:** N/A

**Experiments:**
- Grammar judgement

**Log-likelihood:**

$$
 p(c_t=i) \propto \exp(\Theta_{t,i})
$$

where $\Theta \in \mathbb{R}^{T \times N_c}$ are free parameters.
